items.title: OpenAI Unveils Next Evolution of Agents SDK for Safer, More Capable Enterprise AI Agents
items.summary: OpenAI has updated its Agents SDK, focusing on enhancing safety and capabilities for enterprises building AI agents, signaling a move towards more robust and reliable AI deployments.

items.title: Google Introduces Gemini 3.1 Flash TTS for Expressive AI Speech
items.summary: Google has launched Gemini 3.1 Flash TTS, the latest generation of its text-to-speech model, promising more expressive and natural-sounding AI-generated speech.

items.title: ClawGUI: A Unified Framework for Training, Evaluating, and Deploying GUI Agents
items.summary: Researchers introduce ClawGUI, a new unified framework designed to streamline the training, evaluation, and deployment of AI agents that interact with graphical user interfaces.

items.title: KnowRL: Boosting LLM Reasoning with Reinforcement Learning and Knowledge Guidance
items.summary: A new research paper presents KnowRL, a method that leverages reinforcement learning with minimal-sufficient knowledge guidance to significantly improve the reasoning capabilities of Large Language Models.

items.title: Hightouch Achieves $100M ARR, Driven by AI-Powered Marketing Tools
items.summary: Hightouch has reached a significant milestone of $100 million in Annual Recurring Revenue, largely attributed to the success and adoption of its AI-powered marketing tools.

items.title: Rethinking On-Policy Distillation of Large Language Models: Phenomenology, Mechanism, and Recipe
items.summary: New research delves into the intricacies of on-policy distillation for large language models, offering insights into its mechanisms and proposing an improved recipe for more effective training.

items.title: LinkedIn Data Suggests AI Not Yet Responsible for Hiring Decline
items.summary: New data from LinkedIn indicates that, contrary to some concerns, AI is not currently the primary cause of a decline in hiring, though its future impact remains a subject of observation.

items.title: Nemotron 3 Super: Open, Efficient Mixture-of-Experts Hybrid Model for Agentic Reasoning
items.summary: A new paper introduces Nemotron 3 Super, an open and efficient mixture-of-experts model combining Mamba and Transformer architectures, designed to enhance agentic reasoning capabilities.

items.title: Toward Autonomous Long-Horizon Engineering for ML Research
items.summary: Researchers are exploring methods for autonomous long-horizon engineering, aiming to enable AI systems to independently conduct and manage complex machine learning research projects.

items.title: Lyra 2.0: Explorable Generative 3D Worlds Unveiled
items.summary: Lyra 2.0 is introduced as a new system for creating explorable generative 3D worlds, pushing the boundaries of immersive virtual environment generation.

items.title: How to Prompt Gemini 3.1's New Text-to-Speech Model for Optimal Results
items.summary: A guide has been published detailing effective prompting techniques for Google's new Gemini 3.1 text-to-speech model, helping developers achieve high-quality expressive audio.

items.title: Audio-Omni: Extending Multi-modal Understanding to Versatile Audio Generation and Editing
items.summary: Audio-Omni is a new development that expands multi-modal AI understanding to include versatile audio generation and editing capabilities, bridging the gap between text, image, and sound.

items.title: AI Assistance Reduces Persistence and Hurts Independent Performance
items.summary: A new study suggests that while AI assistance can be helpful, it may inadvertently reduce users' persistence and negatively impact their ability to perform tasks independently.

items.title: BERT-as-a-Judge: A Robust Alternative for Efficient Reference-Based LLM Evaluation
items.summary: Researchers propose 'BERT-as-a-Judge' as a robust and efficient alternative to traditional lexical methods for evaluating Large Language Models based on reference texts.

items.title: The Blind Spot of Agent Safety: How Benign User Instructions Expose Critical Vulnerabilities in Computer-Use Agents
items.summary: A study highlights critical safety vulnerabilities in computer-use agents, revealing how seemingly benign user instructions can inadvertently expose significant security risks.
